summary: The diff shows backend implementation (backend/main.mo) with Super Admin initialization and recovery logic, but does NOT implement the frontend recovery UI required by REQ-2. The backend uses a dynamic initialization approach instead of hardcoding the specified principal ID.

missing_requirements.requirement: Hardcode the principal ID 'fiawz-svdcq-fsplo-srina-bia3r-kdxu5-c4tcj-e7bkm-w3gex-cvpvm-2qe' as the Super Admin principal in backend/main.mo
missing_requirements.reason: The backend uses a dynamic initialization approach where superAdminPrincipal is set to ?caller in initializeSuperAdmin(), rather than hardcoding the specified principal ID. No evidence of the literal principal string 'fiawz-svdcq-fsplo-srina-bia3r-kdxu5-c4tcj-e7bkm-w3gex-cvpvm-2qe' appears in the backend code.

missing_requirements.requirement: Add a Super Admin recovery mechanism in the AdminDashboardPage that displays a recovery button or automatic recovery flow
missing_requirements.reason: No changes to AdminDashboardPage or any frontend files are shown in the diff. Only backend/main.mo and project_state.json were modified. The frontend UI components for recovery (button, automatic flow, success/failure feedback) are completely missing.

missing_requirements.requirement: After successful recovery, the user's role updates to Super Admin without requiring re-authentication
missing_requirements.reason: Without frontend implementation, there is no mechanism to update the user's role in the UI state after calling recoverSuperAdmin(). The backend recoverSuperAdmin() only returns a text confirmation message, not a role update.

hallucinated_features.description: Implemented initializeSuperAdmin() method that allows any authenticated caller to become Super Admin if not yet initialized
hallucinated_features.reason: The BuildRequest specifies hardcoding a specific principal ID, not creating a first-caller-wins initialization system. This approach allows any principal to claim Super Admin if they call initializeSuperAdmin() first, which contradicts the security requirements.

hallucinated_features.description: Added getSuperAdminStatus() query method to check Super Admin initialization state
hallucinated_features.reason: This method was not requested in the BuildRequest. While potentially useful, it represents additional functionality beyond the specified requirements.
